greeting: Well hello there, fellow pixel-pushers and plugin aficionados!
introParagraph: This week's 'Release Radar' pings are coming in hot, primarily from the form and automation front. We saw a flurry of activity, with some critical fixes squashing pesky payment bugs and a few neat features making their debut. It seems like the developers were busy ensuring everything's running smoothly under the hood, with a sprinkle of new capabilities to keep things fresh.

highlights.title: WP Simple Pay 4.17.1
highlights.summary: WP Simple Pay rolled out a handy new installment description format setting, giving you more control over how recurring payments appear. This means clearer communication for your customers and better organization for you, configurable both globally and per form.

highlights.title: Uncanny Automator 7.2.0
highlights.summary: Uncanny Automator beefed up its AffiliateWP integration, introducing new triggers based on an affiliate's unpaid referral count and visit count. This opens up exciting new possibilities for automating your affiliate program workflows and keeping an even closer eye on performance.

highlights.title: WPForms Lite 1.10.0.4
highlights.summary: WPForms delivered a crucial fix for Stripe payments with Link and 3D Secure cards, addressing an issue where the authentication modal wasn't triggering. This ensures smoother, more reliable payment processing and prevents incomplete transactions, which is always a win!

highlights.title: Charitable 1.8.10.4
highlights.summary: Charitable swooped in with an important fix addressing an accumulation of multiple Stripe webhook endpoints, which could previously cause donation webhook events to fail signature verification. This update ensures the integrity and reliability of your donation processing.

closingJoke: Why did the WordPress developer break up with the JavaScript framework? Because he needed more commitment from his dependencies! At least our plugins are committed to regular updates.
